subject: Chemistry
title: Hard Water and Limescale
description: One-minute skill: Hard Water and Limescale.

hook.text: That white crusty buildup on your faucet is calcium carbonate—the same material as seashells.

buildup.text: Hard water contains dissolved calcium and magnesium ions picked up from underground limestone.

discovery.text: When hard water is heated, calcium bicarbonate decomposes into calcium carbonate, water, and CO₂.

twist.text: Vinegar dissolves limescale because acetic acid reacts with calcium carbonate, turning it back into solution.

climax.text: Water softeners swap calcium ions for sodium ions. Soft water makes more suds and leaves no scale.

punchline.text: Dissolved rock in your water. Heat it, and the rock comes back.

quiz.question: What causes limescale buildup in kettles and faucets?
options: Dissolved calcium in hard water deposits as solid calcium carbonate when heated
Iron in old pipes rusts and flakes off
